danger_signals.visual_form: Palette-shifted enemy sprites appearing on screen; multiple enemy types with distinct silhouettes
danger_signals.meaning: Escalating threat level; different enemy palettes indicate regional criminal organization diversity

danger_signals.visual_form: Larger enemy sprites, distinctive character designs (e.g., Callman); can appear at multiple stage locations
danger_signals.meaning: Major threat encounter; bosses represent Skull Cross Gang leadership hierarchy

danger_signals.visual_form: Downtown stage environments with destruction indicators; Skull Cross Gang activity in multiple city zones
danger_signals.meaning: Organized criminal threat has spread throughout Metro City; systemic danger rather than isolated incident

danger_signals.visual_form: Barrels and other destructible containers scattered throughout stages
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental instability; weapons and items may be hidden, requiring active search for survival resources

safety_signals.visual_form: Food items stored in breakable drums/containers
safety_signals.meaning: Recovery opportunity; safe zones where supplies can be obtained
Stable environment zone where resources can be safely gathered

safety_signals.visual_form: Guy reunites with Mike Haggar (Mayor); presence of allied characters Lucia and Dean
safety_signals.meaning: Institutional support restored; legitimate authority (Mayor) and law enforcement (Detective Lucia) present

faction_indicators.color_identity: Palette variations used to differentiate enemy types and regional variants
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Skull Cross (implicit in gang name); enemy visual identity through distinct sprite designs
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Multiple enemy types with different palette assignments; same enemy type (e.g., Andore) appears in multiple color variants throughout stages, suggesting distributed organization
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Presence throughout multiple stage zones (downtown, sewer areas in Round 5); riot activities in downtown district indicate territorial control

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Mayor Mike Haggar; Detective Lucia (Special Crimes Unit)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Police Special Crimes Unit official; Mayor presence indicates institutional response to gang threat

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Post-Mad Gear Gang era; power vacuum filled
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: New criminal organization (Skull Cross Gang) has emerged with distributed presence across Metro City zones
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Active rioting in downtown; organized criminal activity requiring multi-stage intervention

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Multiple stages featuring different zone types (downtown, sewer, industrial areas); environmental variety indicates gang territorial expansion
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Branching stage paths suggest player agency in choosing combat routes; multiple boss encounters at different locations reflect decentralized gang structure

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Protagonist Guy returns from martial arts training; four playable characters (heroes) deployed
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Coordinated response with Mayor Haggar, Detective Lucia, and street fighter Dean alongside Guy

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Enemy palette diversity indicates multiple gang subdivisions or regional variations; Andore enemy appears in multiple color variants
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Different enemy types with unique behaviors; boss encounters vary by stage path taken

environmental_storytelling.example: Breakable barrels and drums contain weapons, health items, and bonus points; Yashichi invincibility item hidden in foreground objects (partially visible in sewer stage Round 5)
Six unique stages with distinct visual themes (downtown, sewer); background details tell story of Metro City infrastructure and gang activity zones
Each playable character specializes in specific weapon type (Guy uses nunchaku); weapon selection from environment reflects character-specific combat identity
Multiple stage routes change areas visited and bosses encountered (e.g., Callman appears at two different locations depending on path); environmental layout communicates player choice consequences
Same enemy type (Andore) appears in multiple palette variants across stages; visual differentiation suggests gang hierarchy, regional divisions, or threat escalation
Downtown riot setting; sewer stage environment; stage-specific background details (unseen due to black bars in original display, but designed to establish location identity)

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Dash attacks, dashing jump attacks, behind-grab throws, directional lock-facing; indicates Street Fighter-style input complexity and modernized combat system
Appear at specific stage locations; larger sprite size; unique character designs; can be encountered at multiple locations depending on branching path
Multiple types with distinct silhouettes; palette variants; spawn patterns indicate coordinated gang activity
Fills during regular attacks; auto-empties if not used within time window; powerful special attack upon full gauge; indicates momentum-based threat assessment
Breakable containers yield weapons, health, bonus items; resources tied to environmental destruction

notes: Final Fight 3 communicates world-state primarily through enemy palette variation (indicating Skull Cross Gang diversity and scale), environmental stage design (showing territorial expansion), and branching path mechanics (reflecting player agency in response to distributed threat). The visual shift from single Mad Gear Gang threat to multiple Skull Cross Gang subdivisions (evidenced by palette variants and multi-location bosses) signals a more decentralized criminal structure. Character specialization (unique weapons, moves) and support system (CPU partner, multiple playable characters) visually communicate institutional response escalation. Sewer and downtown environments establish gang presence across infrastructure. The game uses visual complexity (multiple paths, varied enemy types, branching bosses) rather than explicit narrative exposition to communicate shifting political control in Metro City.
